rules.label: Undue Emphasis on Significance, Legacy, and Broader Trends
rules.detection_hint: \b(stands? as|serves? as|marks? a pivotal|testament to|underscores? (the|its)|highlights? (the|its) (importance|significance)|shaping the|reflects? broader|setting the stage|indelible mark|deeply rooted|focal point|enduring legacy)\b
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when two or more significance-inflation phrases appear in a single paragraph, inflating ordinary facts into landmark claims.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'This initiative marks a pivotal moment in the evolution of regional governance.' → After: 'This initiative changed how the region approves infrastructure projects.'

rules.label: Undue Emphasis on Notability and Media Coverage
rules.detection_hint: \b(featured in|covered by|as reported by|cited in|independent coverage|active social media presence|over \d[\d,]+ followers|written by a leading expert)\b
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when fabricated or unverifiable media claims are present (outlet names cannot be confirmed against preserve_facts).
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'Her work has been featured in The New York Times with 500,000 followers.' → After: 'Her work has appeared in national outlets and attracted a large online readership.' (only if verifiable)

rules.label: Superficial Analyses with -ing Endings
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when the trailing participial clause is the only analytical content in the sentence (no concrete supporting detail).
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'The temple uses blue and gold colors, symbolizing the region's deep connection to the land.' → After: 'The temple uses blue and gold — the region's traditional ceremonial colors.'

rules.label: Promotional and Advertisement-like Language
rules.when_high: Always high in technical or academic domains; high in any domain when the promotional term replaces a specific factual claim.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'Nestled within the breathtaking region of Gonder, the town boasts a vibrant cultural heritage.' → After: 'Gonder is a historic walled town in northern Ethiopia, site of six 17th-century castles.'

rules.label: Vague Attributions and Weasel Words
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when the vague attribution is the sole support for a causal or statistical claim.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'Experts believe the river plays a crucial role in the regional ecosystem.' → After: 'The river supplies irrigation for 40% of the region's farmland (Ministry of Agriculture, 2023).'

rules.label: Outline-like Challenges and Future Prospects Sections
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when the section contains no specific challenges, only abstract nouns (e.g., 'faces challenges of complexity and scale').
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'Despite its success, the city faces several challenges. Despite these challenges, growth continues.' → After: 'The city's main constraint is water supply — current capacity covers 70% of peak demand.'

rules.label: Overused AI Vocabulary Words
rules.when_high: High when three or more distinct vocabulary words from the list appear in a 200-character window, or when 'delve' or 'tapestry' appear anywhere.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'Additionally, the enduring testament to Italian influence showcases the crucial interplay.' → After: 'Italian influence is visible in the arcade facades and the grid street plan.'

rules.label: Avoidance of is/are (Copula Avoidance)
rules.when_high: High when copula-avoidance constructions appear more than twice in a paragraph, making the prose consistently evasive.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'Gallery 825 serves as LAAA's exhibition space and boasts over 3,000 square feet.' → After: 'Gallery 825 is LAAA's exhibition space — 3,000 square feet.'

rules.label: Negative Parallelisms
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when the negative-parallelism construction is the thesis sentence and the positive clause adds no specific information.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'It's not just about the beat; it's part of the overall aggression and atmosphere.' → After: 'The beat drives the track's aggression.'

rules.label: Rule of Three Overuse
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when two or more triadic lists appear within 300 characters and all items are abstract nouns.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'The event features innovation, inspiration, and industry insights.' → After: 'The event covers three topics: [specific topic A], [specific topic B], [specific topic C].'

rules.label: Elegant Variation (Synonym Cycling)
rules.detection_hint: Within 300 chars: 3+ distinct noun phrases referring to the same entity (protagonist|main character|central figure|hero|the lead)
rules.when_high: Escalate to high in technical documentation where synonym cycling causes ambiguity about which entity is being referenced.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'The protagonist faces obstacles. The central figure triumphs. The hero returns.' → After: 'Alicia faces the obstacle, overcomes it, and returns.'

rules.label: False Ranges
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when the false range spans incompatible conceptual categories (e.g., metaphysical to physical) making the sentence meaningless.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'From the singularity of the Big Bang to the cosmic web, from the birth of stars to dark matter.' → After: 'The model spans cosmological scales, from the Big Bang to present-day large-scale structure.'

rules.label: Em Dash Overuse
rules.when_high: High when more than two em dashes appear in a 300-character window or more than one appears in a single sentence.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'The term is promoted by Dutch institutions—not by the people themselves—yet this mislabeling continues—even in official documents.' → After: 'The term comes from Dutch institutions, not from the people themselves, and persists in official documents.'

rules.label: Overuse of Boldface
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when bold spans appear in consecutive sentences or more than three times in a 300-character block, creating visual noise.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'It blends **OKRs**, **KPIs**, and **Balanced Scorecard (BSC)** into **a unified framework**.' → After: 'It combines OKRs, KPIs, and Balanced Scorecard into one framework.'

rules.label: Inline-Header Vertical Lists
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when three or more consecutive bullets follow the inline-header pattern with no prose bridging them.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: '- **User Experience:** The UX has been improved.' → After: Integrate into prose or use a proper section heading with paragraph text.

rules.label: Title Case in Headings
rules.when_high: Escalate to high in academic or technical documents where style guide specifies sentence case for section headings.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: '## Strategic Negotiations And Global Partnerships' → After: '## Strategic negotiations and global partnerships'

rules.label: Emojis
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when emojis appear in section headings or document titles in formal/technical/academic contexts.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: '🚀 **Launch Phase:** The product launches in Q3' → After: '**Launch Phase:** The product launches in Q3'

rules.label: Curly Quotation Marks
rules.when_high: Escalate to high in code documentation or markdown files where curly quotes break syntax highlighting or copy-paste fidelity.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: '“the project is on track”' → After: '"the project is on track"'

rules.label: Collaborative Communication Artifacts
rules.detection_hint: \b(I hope (this|that) helps|let me know if you('d| would) like|here is (a|an|the|your)|of course!|certainly!|feel free to|would you like me to|is there anything else)\b
rules.when_high: Always high — these phrases are unambiguous chatbot artifacts with no place in any professional document.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'Here is an overview of the French Revolution. I hope this helps!' → After: Remove the opener; begin directly with 'The French Revolution (1789–1799) ended the monarchy...'

rules.label: Knowledge-Cutoff Disclaimers
rules.detection_hint: \b(as of (my )?(last |knowledge )?(update|cutoff|training)|up to my (last )?training|based on available information|while specific details (are|remain) (limited|scarce)|it (appears?|seems?) to have been)\b
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when the disclaimer appears in a document presented as authoritative or published (e.g., a report, spec, or press release).
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'While specific details are limited based on available information, the company appears to have been founded in the 1990s.' → After: State the known fact or omit; do not hedge with AI-specific uncertainty language.

rules.label: Sycophantic/Servile Tone
rules.detection_hint: \b(great question!|excellent (point|question)|you('re| are) absolutely right|that('s| is) (an )?excellent|good point!|what a (great|thoughtful))\b
rules.when_high: Always high — sycophantic openers are unambiguous AI artifacts that undermine the author's credibility in any context.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'Great question! You're absolutely right that this is a complex topic.' → After: Delete the opener and begin with the substantive response.

rules.label: Filler Phrases
rules.detection_hint: \b(in order to|due to the fact that|at this point in time|in the event that|has the ability to|it is important to note that|it should be noted that|needless to say|as a matter of fact|for all intents and purposes)\b
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when multiple filler phrases stack in a single sentence, creating a phrase-density pattern associated with AI generation.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'In order to achieve this goal, it is important to note that due to the fact that resources are limited, planning is key.' → After: 'To hit this goal with limited resources, plan early.'

rules.label: Excessive Hedging
rules.when_high: Escalate to high when three or more hedge tokens appear within a 100-character window (stacked hedging).
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'It could potentially possibly be argued that the policy might have some effect.' → After: 'The policy likely reduced costs by 12% in the first quarter (see Table 2).'

rules.label: Generic Positive Conclusions
rules.detection_hint: \b(the future (looks|is) bright|exciting times (lie|are) ahead|continue(s)? (this|our|the) journey (toward|towards)|(in )?(the )?right direction|we look forward to|a (major|significant) step forward|the possibilities are endless|sky'?s the limit)\b
rules.when_high: High when appearing in the final 20% of any document — generic conclusions are a near-universal AI tell that erases the author's actual position.
rules.suggested_fix: Before: 'The future looks bright. Exciting times lie ahead as we continue this journey toward excellence.' → After: State the actual next action, deadline, or open question instead.
